properties.body:
NAVAREA XIX 96/23
 SINCE 2010, THE FIVE ARCTIC NAV- AND METAREAS HAVE PROMULGATED WARNINGS AND FORECASTS TO THEIR AREAS OF RESPONSIBILITY USING INMARSAT-C’S RECTANGULAR AREA ADDRESSING CAPABILITY. THIS BECAUSE IN 2010, SOME OLDER INMARSAT-C RECEIVERS IN USE COULD NOT BE CODED TO RECEIVE WARNINGS AND FORECASTS DIRECTLY ADDRESSED TO THE ARCTIC NAV- AND METAREAS.
  
 ALL INMARSAT-C AND MINI-C RECEIVERS AVAILABLE FOR PURCHASE TODAY CAN RECEIVE WARNINGS AND FORECASTS ADDRESSED TO THE ARCTIC NAV- AND METEAREAS, AND HAVE BEEN ABLE TO DO SO FOR MORE THAN A DECADE. 
  
 EFFECTIVE ON JANUARY 1ST, 2024, THE ARCTIC NAV- AND METAREAS WILL COMMENCE BROADCASTING THEIR WARNINGS AND FORECASTS USING THE NUMERIC NAV- OR METAREA IDENTIFIER AND WILL CEASE USING RECTANGULAR ADDRESSING.
  
 DURING 2023 THE ARCTIC NAV- AND METAREAS WILL BROADCAST TEST MESSAGES USING THE NUMERIC NAV- OR METAREA IDENTIFIER TO ABLE MARINERS TO CHECK THEIR INMARSAT-C OR MINI-C RECEIVERS.
  
 MARINERS NAVIGATING IN ARCTIC WATERS MUST ENSURE THAT THEIR INMARSAT-C OR MINI-C RECEIVER RECOGNIZE THE NEW ENCODING PRIOR TO JANUARY 1ST, 2024, IN ORDER TO CONTINUING RECEIVING ARCTIC NAV- AND METAREA WARNINGS AND FORECASTS.
